behavior_summary: Provides the gameplay behavior for a raise/lower platform item: players can 'U8P_PLATFORM::use' the platform or heavy items hitting it can trigger an activation that runs a worker to animate the platform and move stacked items upward.

PLATFORM::use inspects the platform's current quality/state and surface weight to decide whether to spawn a worker process (PLATFORM::0131) that performs the raise/lower animation, or to play a failure sound if activation conditions are not met.

PLATFORM::gotHit reacts to damage/impact events: it ignores zero hits, otherwise checks if the referenced item is on the platform and if that item's weight (including contents) meets a threshold; if so it triggers the same U8P_PLATFORM::use behavior (spawning the worker) so heavy impacts can cause the platform to operate.

PLATFORM::0131 is the worker that carries out the animated transition: it sets intermediate/final quality states on the platform, plays SFX at key moments, computes effective altitude, iterates the ethereal/stack above the platform to pop/push or ascend items (including mapping item visuals/types by altitude), and spawns a cleanup/free process when finished.
notable_control_flow: Entry via U8P_PLATFORM::PLATFORM::use checks Item::getQuality. If quality == 1 or 2 it spawns U8P_PLATFORM::PLATFORM::0131 with different initial flags. Otherwise it reads Item::getSurfaceWeight and activates the worker only if weight <= threshold (0x64), else plays a failure SFX and returns.

U8P_PLATFORM::PLATFORM::gotHit conditionally spawns U8P_PLATFORM::PLATFORM::use when a hit is non-zero, the referenced item is 'on' the platform (Item::isOn), and its weight including contents >= threshold (0x0A) — otherwise it adjusts an internal counter and suspends.

U8P_PLATFORM::PLATFORM::0131 sets a transient quality/state, loops to inspect and move stacked items up a Z-level (using getEtherealTop, pop/push, Item::ascend), remaps visual/type placeholders based on computed Z ranges, plays SFX on transitions, then spawns FREE::1C19 for cleanup and suspends.
open_questions: Exact semantics and full set of numeric 'quality' codes (beyond inferred 1/2 as activatable states and intermediate codes used by the worker).
Precise meaning of the initial flag byte passed to PLATFORM::0131 (0, 1, 0xFE observed) and how each variant affects altitude calculations and visual mapping.
Exact SFX ids and their intended audio cues (success, failure, transition).
How Item::ascend failure modes are handled in detail (fall-back pop/push behavior) and whether other engine rules (collisions, blocking) influence the worker.
Whether other external events/processes can preempt the worker or affect intermediate quality values while PLATFORM::0131 runs.

main_function.name: U8P_PLATFORM::use
main_function.purpose: Handle player/actor 'U8P_PLATFORM::use' interaction with the platform item. Decide whether conditions (quality or surface weight) permit starting the platform animation/worker; if permitted spawn U8P_PLATFORM::PLATFORM::0131 with an initial flag, otherwise play a failure SFX.
entry_conditions: Invoked when a player or actor uses/interacts with the platform item (U8P_PLATFORM::use event).

function_map.name: U8P_PLATFORM::use
function_map.role: Entry handler for 'U8P_PLATFORM::use' interactions; gatekeeper that decides whether to spawn the platform worker or play failure sound.

function_map.name: U8P_PLATFORM::gotHit
function_map.role: Responds to hit/damage events; triggers the U8P_PLATFORM::use behavior when a heavy-enough item on the platform is hit.

function_map.name: U8P_PLATFORM::0131
function_map.role: Worker/process that animates the platform transition: set qualities, play SFX, move/push/pop/ascend stacked items, remap visual types, and spawn cleanup.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the worker that performs the platform raise/lower animation and payload movement; caller chooses initial flag based on quality or surface weight.
Trigger the U8P_PLATFORM::use handler (and thus potential spawning of the worker) when a heavy item on the platform is hit.
Spawn a cleanup/free process after the worker finishes moving items and updating platform state.

external_calls.purpose: Read platform 'quality' state to decide activation path and flag to pass to the worker.
Read weight on the platform to allow activation when quality is not already the activatable codes; used as a guard threshold.
Play a failure SFX when activation conditions are not met (surface weight too high) or play-start SFX when spawning the worker.
Update platform quality/state codes to mark intermediate and final animation states.
Obtain platform X coordinate to operate on the stack at that location (pop/push operations).
Obtain platform Y coordinate to operate on the stack at that location (pop/push operations).
Read platform Z to compute effective altitude and choose visual/type mappings and quality transitions.
Find the topmost ethereal/stack reference so the worker can iterate items above the platform and move them upward.
Inspect each stacked item's type to map it to a placeholder or alternate visual type depending on altitude.
Insert placeholder or new stack entries while moving items during the raise animation.
Remove items from stack positions (variants used to pop at x,y,z) as part of lifting/moving payload.
Attempt to move items (or the platform/payload) up one Z-level; return value influences worker behavior for each item.
Verify the referenced item is located on this platform before using its weight to decide activation.
Measure total weight of a referenced item (including its contents) to compare against the activation threshold.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the gameplay behavior for a raise/lower platform item: players can 'U8P_PLATFORM::use' the platform or heavy items hitting it can trigger an activation that runs a worker to animate the platform and move stacked items upward.
